reviews.title: A beautiful oasis in a bustling city!
reviews.positives: The property is located far from the old quarter and the noise. The rooms on the executive floor are fantastic and the lounge is great. I was there for two stays in two weeks; over 9 days. I cannot say enough about how good the food is in the Marriott! Part of my trip was cancelled due to the historic flooding in Hoi An at the end of October. Lyn Do, a staff member in the lounge, was very generous with his time and expertise. He helped me make a new plan and make the most of my time in his country.
reviews.negatives: In low traffic it is a 30 minute cab ride and in high traffic it can be well over an hour. That said, I still loved it! I went into the old quarter often but craved the peace of this hotel at night!

reviews.title: Away from the maddening crowd
reviews.text: The Marriott is located far from the old quarter where most of the attractions are located. The do however more than make up for this by a thrice daily shuttle to 4 tourist spots like the water puppet theater at the Hoan Kiem lake, Hoa Lao prison, Ho Chi Minh mausoleum and so on but you have to catch a one way can ride back. Their tourist desk will give friendly advice on which cab companies to take and which to avoid. They even have taxi cards in Vietnamese to provide instructions to drivers and a handy free Hanoi guide book. We stayed in room 240 a lake facing suite although paid for a city facing suite. They must have quietly upgraded us. Room was HUGE. 3 tvs, 2 bathrooms, 2 room sized closets- the size of a small apartment! Impressed! Check in was speedy at the club level (6). Club access was definitely a plus with 3 meals per day if you chose to eat in. The  hotel boasts the second best restaurant in Hanoi and has a crystal Jade palace restaurant for Chinese cuisine. Swimming pool is indoor on the 8th floor but it is small considering the size of the hotel. Overall a Pleasant experience and would return if staying again in Hanoi

reviews.title: It's not the same feeling as before.
reviews.text: Overall, I was satisfied. However, I was very disappointed during breakfast. Before my wedding, I received good service here and had good memories, so this time I stayed for one night with my baby to celebrate Children's Day. Firstly, there wasn't a table for the whole family to sit together during breakfast, so we were guided to two tables that seated two each. It was uncomfortable to have a meal with a baby, but since there were many guests, we had no choice but to use those tables. When I went to get more food, our table had been cleared. I had placed baby wipes on the table just in case... I was flustered holding a plate of food and the baby's hand. They said it was difficult to guide us due to a lack of available seats, so we asked the guests at the adjacent table for their understanding and awkwardly moved our chairs closer together. I asked them to guide us if a table became available again, and when we moved, the newly cleaned table had a sour odor from the cloth used for cleaning. They took action to address it, but that day I saw several complaints at nearby tables similar to ours (cleared tables before finishing meals). Why has the JW restaurant come to this? I remembered the food being delicious, but unfortunately, I couldn't really taste it and left the restaurant with only discomfort. Other than that, I was generally satisfied with the service from the spa and the café garden staff on the lobby floor. In particular, the lobby café had excellent beverage and bakery service and a great view, so I will visit again. However, I'm concerned about whether to stay and use the main restaurant.
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Customer,
We are delighted to have been your chosen hotel again. It was truly a pleasure to serve you and your family. 
It has come to our attention that the service you received during your recent stay did not meet your expectations. For this, we sincerely apologize. 
The rating you shared is not characteristic of the level of service our associates strive to provide. We have shared your feedback with the Restaurant Manager and as a teachable moment, we have discussed our service standards and reviewed improvement opportunities in the service we provide. We are confident that these necessary steps will ensure your experience is an isolated incident.
Again, allow us to reiterate our sincere apologies for any inconvenience during your stay and we hope you choose to stay with us again when your travels bring you back to the area. 
Sincerely,
My Pham
Quality Manager

reviews.text: At first, I used the floor with the lounge, but there was a strong mold smell in the hallway. The smell continued in the room, and upon inspection, I found water leaking by the window and mold on the ceiling. The mold odor was so severe that when I requested a change, they promptly gave me a different room. The staff is friendly. However, compared to the long layout of the accommodation, there is only one elevator, which makes it very inconvenient to walk quite a distance. It is much more expensive compared to other hotels in Hanoi, and aside from the friendliness of the staff, it was not very impressive.

However, all three rooms inspected for the change had the same issue... So ultimately, I switched to a balcony room and left the balcony door open for a while to ventilate, which helped a little. No matter how much it rained the day before, this was quite excessiveㅠㅠ The food in the lounge was delicious and the staff was very attentive, but the smell in the room was a significant negative point... It doesn't always seem to be like this, but for those who are sensitive to such issues or are staying with young children, I recommend taking note!

reviews.text: This is an excellent hotel and I had an amazing stay. Most of the staff are outstanding but a couple of staff let the others down and I could see that there was some poor supervision from more senior managers who were not on the ball. My one request is that it appears that the hotel has its own dedicated taxi drivers, please ask them not to keep using the car horn. I know all the other drivers use it constantly in Hanoi the noise of which makes staying in Hanoi bothersome but I found asking the drivers I had not to do it made my journeys much more pleasant. My final taxi driver seemed quite amazed that he could actually get to the airport without using it!

reviews.positives:
Die Zimmer waren stets sehr sauber und baten all den Komfort den man sich wünschen kann. Alle möglichen internationalen Kanäle bat das Fernsehen und der Wifi war sehr stabil. 

Was mir aber besonders gefallen hat sind die Mitarbeiter. Alle samt beste manieren, sehr freundlich, entgegenkommend, lächelnd und alle in ihrem Fach voller Expertise sei es das Essen, Getränke Herkunft/Story oder auch bei kulturellen Fragen meinerseits. Das JW Marriott Management hat hier wirklich sehr gute Arbeit geleistet!. 
Besonderer Dank geht noch an Tuan von der Bar und der Trainee Anh, ich sehe hier sehr viel Potentiall in Zukunft. Aber ein generelles Danke an das ganze Team! Eines der besten Hotel Erlebnisse die ich hatte und ich habe wirklich sehr viele 5Sterne Hotels besucht in den letzten Jahren.
reviews.negatives: Das man gewaschene Wäsche vor der Tür oder im Zimmer bezahlen muss, laut Housekeeping Manager wäre das ein JWM Standard. Falls das der Fall dann würde ich dem Management raten dies zu ändern. Weil ich mehrere Tage auf meine Wäsche gewartet habe in Erwartung sie würde im Kleiderschrank hängen oder meinetwegen vor der Tür. Die Lage lässt die Fahrt in die Altstadt etwas lang ausfallen wegen dem meist doch chaotischem Verkehr. Aber im großen und ganzen war ich sehr zu frieden.

reviews.title: Brand New Hotel, Great Business Hotel
reviews.text:
In Hanoi for business, I wanted to be near the modern and business side of Hanoi. There is a sort of highway straight from the airport bypassing the busy traffic parts of Hanoi.it is also next to the convention centre in away from the buildings around... 

It is far away from the tourist side of Hanoi, the old quarters. Location wise, it was great... unless you are a tourist.

Being new, the facilities were in good condition... great gym and lap pool at the roof top... the room was modern and well appointed.

Breakfast at the restaurant had a good international and local spread... the different stations were all over... but that also meant no congestions. Service staff were attentive and quick to clear the tables... without being intrusive, young and eager to serve, could tell that they were well trained.

This is one international hotel that probably lived up to its 5 star billing and perhaps a bit more. Would certainly plan to stay again the next time, not least because it was near where I needed to go for my meetings.

reviews.title: Great hotel, away from city centre
reviews.text:
The Marriott is everything to would expect. Big, modern, well-run, efficient. Great staff.

Rooms - pretty much perfect, with everything you might possibly need.

Food and drink - breakfast is awesome; you could spend all day there! Similarly the evening buffet dinner is impressive, but not cheap, even by Western standards. The main bar is pretty cool, with resident lounge DJs adding to the atmosphere.

Leisure - gym is very good. Pool is covered, so no chance to enjoy a day in the sunshine by the pool. I was disappointed by this. Also, runners should note that the inviting looking tracks around the adjacent lake are private. I was chased off there at 7am by a security guy on a moped.

Location - prefect if you want to be near the convention centre, not so perfect if you want to go into the city centre. It's a half-hour drive. And the airport is even further away.

reviews.title: Traveling with a 1-year-old child
reviews.text: The location is not great. It seems to be geared more towards business rather than tourism. It's about a 30-minute drive from the old town, which is the tourist center, so it may be a bit inconvenient for those focused on sightseeing. However, I recommend it for those who want to enjoy a hotel stay. It's clean and the staff is of a high level. I'm not sure if there are staff members who can speak Japanese. English is perfect. The executive lounge is convenient, and I highly recommend using a plan that includes access to it. I arrived a bit early and had some time before check-in, so I was able to spend my time elegantly while enjoying afternoon tea in the lounge. I also had breakfast in the lounge, and I was a bit surprised to find a pack of natto available. It was actually quite delicious. I wonder if there are quite a few Japanese guests? One thing I regret is that I stayed on New Year's Eve, and when I tried to make a reservation at the French restaurant on the day, it was fully booked. This was my mistake, but it's better to make a reservation in advance. I stayed in a lake view room, and the view was great and the size was sufficient. The electric kettle was a bit flimsy, which is the only complaint I have. Overall, I had a pleasant stay. Thank you.

reviews.title: A serene escape from Hanoi's reality
reviews.text: I live in Hanoi and life can get too stressful at times with jampacked streets and crowded public areas so my escape to JW Marriott with my boyfriend was already a luxury. We stayed for a night only, on our first anniversary, (thanks to our demanding jobs) but we both had a great time. We loved French Grill, where we had our anniversary dinner. Man, service was awesome and food was just so yummm! Most of their specials were actually shipped from France and their chef is French too! It was an overall amazing French cuisine experience for us. Then there's Antidote bar with a DJ for our drinks and music after dinner. I also especially liked the 8th floor - swimming pool, juice bar and gym! They're all so spacious and clean. Overall, I'd say JW is inarguably the best hotel I've stayed at in Hanoi (and yes, I've been to all 5 star ones in the city). I wouldn't hesitate to book another stay at any Marriott in the world.

reviews.title: Nice facilities and food
reviews.text: We planned to have dinner at French Grill, the best rated Hanoi restaurant. So we chose a plan without breakfast so as to eat outside and did some sight seeing. But the reception guy offered an irresistible upgrade plan for breakfast and free access to lounge with bottomless afternoon and evening cocktail. Turned out we stayed at the hotel till our late flight the other day. Room, pool and gym could let you spend the day comfotably. We had glasses of wine and cocktail in the evening such that we needed to postponed our dinner. French Grill served really good dishes, both classics and contemporary. Pity that we had too much snacks at lounge. Must go again next time. You couldn't complain about the variety of food at breakfast. But staff could be more flexible in the seating arrangement. We requested to sit by the window but the waitress insisted that the table was for 4 persons though the cafe was just only 10 or 20% ocupied. And a waiter bumped to us in the middle of our meal just to have us sign on the bill. We were told it was the hotel policy to estimate the number of guests therein. But there were plenty of empty tables. Why needed that?
reviews.positives: The decor of the room, indoor pool and lounge was perfect. Staff was friendly and helpful. Food at the French Grill was a must-try.

reviews.title: Excellent business stay
reviews.text:
i recently stayed at JW Marriott Hanoi for a business trip and had a very positive experience.

The hotel is highly suitable for business travelers. The rooms are spacious, modern, and very well-maintained, with a comfortable work desk setup and strong Wi-Fi connection, which made it easy to manage meetings and emails. The bed was exceptionally comfortable, ensuring proper rest after long workdays.

The breakfast spread was impressive, offering a wide variety of international and local options. Service throughout the hotel was professional, efficient, and warm from check-in to concierge assistance. Staff were attentive without being intrusive.

The hotel’s meeting facilities are well-equipped and ideal for corporate events. Its location is also convenient for business meetings around the My Dinh area, while still offering a quiet and peaceful environment away from the busy Old Quarter.

Overall, JW Marriott Hanoi delivers a seamless, comfortable, and premium experience for business travelers. I would definitely stay here again for future work trips.

reviews.title: The Best Place to Stay in Hanoi
reviews.text: After staying in the Old City, we moved to the Marriott and finally got the rest we needed. The staff were friendly, the facilities were clean, and everything was set up for comfort and relaxation. If you value peace and sanity, it’s definitely worth staying in this area—even if it means paying for a ride to the Old City. It was a refreshing change and well worth it.

reviews.positives: Great hospitality and service

reviews.title: Excellent stay
reviews.text:
The JW Marriott Hanoi truly distinguishes itself—with its stunning architecture, expansive layout, and world-class service that rivals the finest resorts.

The property is so vast and beautifully designed, it almost feels like an urban resort—a rare oasis in the heart of the city.

Culinary experiences were on point. Every request was met with precision, including delicious, authentic Indian fare prepared on demand.

We did encounter a minor hiccup with the spa—but the team’s response was exemplary: they offered complimentary spa access the next day, turning a flaw into a highlight.

Special credit to the front desk and concierge teams—their professionalism and attention to detail set a gold standard. Hats off to them.
reviews.response:
Xin chào Narasimha,

We recognize your time is valuable and appreciate the feedback you have provided. We can't thank you enough for the kind words about your experience with JW Marriott Hanoi Hotel. 

Your feedback has been shared with the whole hotel team as a way of celebrating our commitment to providing the highest level of hospitality. 

Wishing you a nice day and look forward to welcoming you back soon to Hanoi.

Sincerely,
Hien Phan
Marketing & Communication Team
